---
title: "nitrix.numerics.fixed_point"
generated: true
signature-hash: 4392b8e0ff7c666e
description: "Fixed-point solver with implicit differentiation."
listing:
  id: nitrix-numerics-fixed_point-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-numerics-fixed_point/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.numerics.fixed_point`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Fixed-point solver with implicit differentiation.

[`fixed_point_solve`](nitrix-numerics-fixed_point/fixed_point_solve.qmd) returns a solution $x^\ast$ satisfying
$x^\ast = f(\mathrm{params}, x^\ast)$ and -- via
`jax.custom_vjp` -- differentiates through the *solution* by the
implicit-function theorem rather than by unrolling the iteration.  At the
fixed point, $\mathrm{d}x^\ast / \mathrm{d}\,\mathrm{params}$
satisfies $(I - \partial_x f)\,\mathrm{d}x^\ast =
\partial_{\mathrm{params}} f$, so the backward pass solves the adjoint
system $w = \bar{g} + (\partial_x f)^{\top} w$ (itself a fixed
point -- a contraction at a stable solution) and pushes $w$ back
through $\partial_{\mathrm{params}} f$.  Memory is $O(1)$ in
the iteration count: the backward needs only $x^\ast$.

The two forward engines share the same implicit-function-theorem adjoint
on the backward pass:

- `acceleration='picard'` (default) -- plain Picard iteration
  $x \leftarrow f(\mathrm{params}, x)$.  Converges geometrically
  with factor $\|\partial_x f\|$; fine when that is comfortably
  below one.
- `acceleration='anderson'` -- windowed Anderson acceleration (Walker
  and Ni), of the given depth.  A quasi-Newton secant mixing of the last
  `depth` residuals; converges where Picard *genuinely* stalls
  ($\|\partial_x f\| \to 1$).  An opt-in escape hatch -- Picard
  is the better choice whenever it converges comfortably (Anderson's
  least-squares over a near-collinear residual history is marginally
  noisier there), which includes the smoothed
  [`invert_displacement`](nitrix-geometry.qmd) /
  [`field_log`](nitrix-geometry.qmd) square root (the half-damping
  already makes the latter super-linear).  The tiny
  $\mathrm{depth} \times \mathrm{depth}$ least-squares is solved
  by an inlined conjugate gradient -- no factorisation, no cuSolver -- so
  it runs inside the traced `while_loop` on every backend.

The named consumers are [`invert_displacement`](nitrix-geometry.qmd) and
[`field_log`](nitrix-geometry.qmd), which are fixed points, and the same
machinery underpins the differentiable-registration story.  It
generalises the scaling-and-squaring special case in
[`integrate_velocity_field`](nitrix-geometry.qmd).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-numerics-fixed_point-symbols}
:::
